channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Salesforce’s Financial Services Cloud targets ops automation for insurance firms
item.description: >To help insurance brokerages tie in disparate systems to manage their operations and increase employee productivity, CRM software provider Salesforce has introduced a new offering in preview, the Financial Services Cloud.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk loaded $75 million into his pro-Trump super PAC in 3 months
item.description: Elon Musk has personally contributed at least $75 million to his pro-Trump America PAC since July as he pushes for the former president to return to the White House, according to new campaign finance records filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: The Viofo A329 Dash Cam: Revolutionizing Road Safety with 4K Video Recording
item.description: The world's first dash cam to feature 4K 60fps video, the Viofo A329 is a game-changer in road safety. Learn more about its advanced features and capabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mila Kunis avoids Ashton Kutcher and Diddy questions as she, Michael Keaton promote ‘Goodrich’
item.description: Kunis' husband, Ashton Kutcher, and their nine-year marriage have faced scrutiny since the Sept. 16 sex trafficking arrest of Kutcher's longtime friend Sean Combs.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Cato Networks extends SASE platform with digital experience monitoring
item.description: Cato Networks today introduced digital experience monitoring (DEM) capabilities that will augment the company’s cloud SASE platform with application traffic and network performance data that IT operations teams need to optimize end-user experiences.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adobe aims to teach AI, online content skills to 30 million
item.description: A new program from Adobe aims to teach 30 million people AI, content creation, and marketing skills by 2030. It’s an extension of the Adobe Digital Academy, which already trains people for new careers in fields like digital marketing, user experience design, and data science.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: More than one-third of cloud environments are critically exposed, says Tenable
item.description: In the first half of this year, 38% of organizations had at least one cloud workload that was critically vulnerable, highly privileged, and publicly exposed, according to a study of telemetry from customers of cloud security vendor Tenable released this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI is killing cloud sustainability
item.description: Sustainability was once a highly touted advantage in public cloud computing. Enterprises and cloud providers flaunted their green initiatives, promoting data centers powered by renewable energy that would reduce carbon footprints. This topic has quietly slipped off the radar in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs
item.description: Michael Berkholtz, a senior manager for technology lifecycle services for GSA, said the agency is analyzing emerging technologies based on four criteria. The post GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM: 78% of mainframe shops are piloting, operating AI apps
item.description: AI is reinvigorating the mainframe and causing enterprises to rethink their plans for mainframe modernization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Volunteers at Concordia Cemetery use technology to unearth Buffalo history
item.description: "We're going to be looking for and bringing up some of the tombstones that have sunk or have been buried," said Bonnie Fleischauer, Concordia Cemetery's public information officer.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation
